# **Bug \#1**

| Field | Details |
| ----- | ----- |
| Bug ID | BUG-001 |
| Title | Create Habit (Valid) Does Not Save to Database |
| Severity | High |
| Priority | P2 |
| Status | Open |
| Reported By | Albert Jhun Cañedo |
| Assigned To | John Mark Paja |
| Date Found | 03/6/2026 |
| Date Fixed | 03/8/2026 |
| GitHub Issue \# | N/A |

## **Description**

TC-007 (Create Habit \- Valid) was marked as FAIL during test execution. When a user fills in all required habit fields and taps Save, the habit is not saved to the SQLite habits table and does not appear on the Dashboard. This is a core CRUD failure affecting the primary feature of the application.

## **Steps to Reproduce**

1. Launch the app and complete onboarding.  
2. Tap the \+ button on the Dashboard to open the Create Habit screen.  
3. Fill in a valid habit title, select a Focus Area, choose an icon and color, then tap Save.

## **Expected vs Actual**

Expected: The habit is inserted into the habits table in SQLite and immediately appears as a card on the Dashboard.

Actual: The habit is not saved. The Dashboard does not show the new habit after tapping Save.

## **Screenshots / Evidence**

## **![][image1]**

## **Environment**

Device: \[DEVICE\]  |  OS: \[VERSION\]  |  Flutter: \[VERSION\]

# **Bug \#2**

| Field | Details |
| ----- | ----- |
| **Bug ID** | BUG-008 |
| **Title** | Delete Account Does Not Clear Any Data — Navigation Only, No Data Deletion Implemented |
| **Severity** | High |
| **Priority** | P1 |
| **Status** | Open |
| **Reported By** | Albert Jhun Cañedo |
| **Assigned To** | John Mark Paja |
| **Date Found** | 03/20/2026 |
| **Date Fixed** | \--- |
| **GitHub Issue \#** | N/A |

## **Description**

## The Delete Account button only redirects users to the Dashboard without removing their data, as the code lacks deletion or reset functions; UserController, DatabaseHelper, and SharedPreferences do not include methods to clear or delete stored information, leaving user data intact despite the intended account deletion.

## **Steps to Reproduce**

4. Launch the app and complete onboarding with a name and at least one habit.  
5. Navigate to the Profile screen.  
6. Tap the Delete Account (or Reset) button and confirm the action.  
7. Observe that the app navigates to the Dashboard or Home screen.  
8. Check that all habits, the user name, and all daily logs are still present — nothing was deleted.  
9. Restart the app and verify the data persists — confirming no deletion occurred.

## **Expected vs Actual**

Expected: Tapping Delete Account clears all rows from the users, habits, daily\_logs, and targets tables, clears SharedPreferences (app\_installation\_date), and navigates the user to the Welcome/Onboarding screen as if the app was freshly installed.

Actual: The app navigates to the Dashboard or Home screen. All user data (name, habits, daily logs, streaks) remains intact. No database tables are cleared, no SharedPreferences keys are removed, and no deletion logic is executed anywhere in the codebase.

## **Screenshots / Evidence**

## **![][image2]![][image3]**

## 

## 

## **Environment**

Device: Physical Android device | OS: Android | Flutter: SDK 3.10.7+ | Files: lib/controllers/user\_controller.dart, lib/services/database\_helper.dart
